description:
A Nice Day for a Game of Golf:
EVA-2 Closeout and the Golf Shots

tokens: And we're probably about 10 minutes away from the LM, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think we're crossing the...Al, here's the...
Triplet right up ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We'll have to do a little bit to the north to get around it, I think.
We're approaching Triplet from the east; that's North Triplet from the east.
(Pause) There's a little rock field down here; a small boulder field, Al, to get a documented sample from?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, looks like they might have come from there (that is, from North Triplet).
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, the shiny can is retrieved.
(Pause) We're going to have to mush, Ed, right down the middle and get a documented sample there.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Right in that pile of rocks; beautiful.
Let's see, right to your left.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: No, I'm trying to stay away from them.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Are these the ones or the ones up ahead?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'm on this side; I'll get the stereo.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Can't really see the camera settings.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Yeah, it's got so much dirt on them.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Ed and Al, as soon as you wrap this one up, you're going to have to press on back to the LM, or we're going to be really tight on the closeout.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: God damn, it's bigger than we thought.
Al, grab-sample that one; I'll get you another one here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Listen, just put it in that thing.
And let's press, because we don't have the time.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'll grab it, and let me take an "after" picture here.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'll grab one right here in the foreground.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And another documented sample...Larger documented sample than we thought we were getting here, Fredo.
Again, it was a buried rock; and it's too big for the sample bag; so, it'll go into a weigh bag.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: It has a very definite shape; I think you'll be able to sort it out.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, let's mush for the LM.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I guess we can skip the rim of North Crater...
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: ...and proceed right on back to the LM area.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We're at the...we are right at the rim of North Crater.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I think you misunderstood the message.
We can proceed right on by the rim.
We have the buried rock samples now, and head on back to the LM.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I'll have it in a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, we're approaching the LM now.
Coming in at Fra Mauro Base.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Roger, Al, and I guess from here, we can split up; and Ed can take the MET and proceed to the cluster of boulders he had reported earlier to the north(west) of the LM; and you can proceed out to the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I'd suggest...Well, you can do it the way you want to...I guess you can do without the LM.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Without the MET, yeah; I think so.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Without the MET, because there's nobody to...If anything falls off, we've lost all those goodies.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'll just take a couple of rock bags, Fredo, and my tongs and camera, and go.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Al's on the way out to the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: As a matter of fact, Fredo, I'm just going to take a weigh bag and no sample bags; that way I can get more.
(Pause) The size of these rocks...The sample bags are too small, anyhow.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Al, the first thing when you get to the Central Station...
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, I'm just going to go through the same procedure.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, and I got a change for you on the azimuth.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: ...on the antenna setup, that is...(Listens) All right.
Let me give you a call when I get there, and when I'm aligned and level.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Fredo, my plan...I'm out in the area of the boulder field.

I'm going to photograph many of the boulders, the rocks, the broken ones, the big ones, what have you; and then, grab as many of the different fragments as I can around these piles of broken boulders.
Now, that I'm here, I see a large number of inclusions.
I can't tell whether they're crystals or not; I think that they are.
And I'll grab as many of these - and give you "before" and "after" shots - as I can...of a whole weigh bag full of rocks.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, the center (means "Central Station") alignment on the ALSEP has changed very little.
There'll be a slight change in the bubble level.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: The setting we need is actually a change in the azimuth reading to sixteen.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, would you verify elevation is still at 6.41?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: You can proceed back to the vicinity of the LM; and, with the time remaining that you had for the ALSEP, shoot a few close-up pictures here.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, a little change in the priorities when you get back to the LM.
We'd like the TV turned to look at the MESA area, so we can watch the closeout (as the) number one (priority).
And then you can shoot a quick picture of the solar wind.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Roger, I'm going for the (TV) camera, now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And we hadn't changed the settings (at the beginning of the traverse), Al; so, it should be in good shape when you turn her to the MESA.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We need a little more to the right, Al.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: I'm just setting it up, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Well, a little more to the left.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, if you can tilt it just up slightly, Al, that'll be it.
That's good; you got good azimuth on it, now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, that's great; and you can go shoot the solar wind, now.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: It (the TV camera)'s on the side of a hill; that's a problem out here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Fredo, I'm heading back from the boulder field.
I've sampled two of the larger boulders in the area.
Rocks broken from them and lying on them; and I've taken a pan; and I have maybe a third of a weigh bag full of small rocks from these boulders.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We need to proceed now with the regular program.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Now back at the foot of the ladder) What setting would you like on that solar wind shot, Fredo?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'd go ahead and use your standard down-Sun picture if that's the direction you're shooting it in.
They don't have an input here.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: They'd like for you to return your camera; so you don't have to bother removing the magazine from it.
You can just put the whole camera in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, and, I guess, so you don't get confused, that means we'll be bringing back both cameras.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Al's camera's in, and magazine Lima-Lima has got a hundred and (pause) nine (frames exposed).
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Reading his checklist at the MET) Okay, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) Okay, I'm putting my camera in the ETB.
(Long Pause as Ed removes his camera from his RCU bracket and then goes to Al's right to get to the ETB)
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (To Al) Let me slide on by you there just a minute.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: What they really wanted was to bring Al's camera back, instead of yours.
So, we'll only be bringing one camera, the CDR's.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Garbled) (At the MET) Fredo, correct me, now; Mag Kilo-Kilo has never been used.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Facing the TV) Houston, while you're looking that up, you might recognize what I have in my hand as the handle for the contingency sample return; it just so happens to have a genuine six iron on the bottom of it.
In my left hand, I have a little white pellet that's familiar to millions of Americans.
Unfortunately, the suit is so stiff, I can't do this with two hands, but I'm going to try a little sand-trap shot here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You got more dirt than ball that time.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: That looked like a slice to me, Al.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Straight as a die; one more.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And (to) answer Ed's question earlier there; Kilo-Kilo was used for the window shots, Ed; so, you ought to bring it back.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: We got some of that to start with, didn't we?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: How many films (means "frames") did we take with this (close-up camera)?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: One additional item on the return is to bring back the 100-foot tether.
That should also go in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Pause) Okay, there's three cassettes...(correcting himself) three frames.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The close-up camera cassette is removed, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, tell me about this (core) tube, Ed.
Has this got anything in it?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: No, that's one that has nothing in it.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Just as Al tosses the empty core tube away) Before you throw it, give them the number.
(Heading out to get the SWC) That's the two (core tube sections out of the triple) that we didn't get anything from.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: In SRC-2, Fredo, we have the organic control sample, and we have four core tubes.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And let's see...(Pause) We have one SESC.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Get it in (the SRC) without dropping it again.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: It should be in the top of the MESA, Ed.
(Long Pause) (To Haise as he goes to the MESA with a weigh bag) Also in the SRC, we have...Maybe it won't fit.
(Pause) One weigh bag, which is mostly documented samples.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (To Ed) That (SWC) supposed to go in here, too?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: No, it goes in the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'll get the core tubes out, maybe.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (So that we can) get the rocks in.
(Long Pause) This baby's what's hurting us.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (At the MET) We didn't get anything in that magnetic sample container, did we?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Also at the MET) No, we did not.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Your feet are about to get tangled up in the TV cable again.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Reading) "Contaminated samples", scratched; "70-millimeter camera mags", yup; "16(-mm) Mags; close-up camera mags; SWC; TDS; magnetic sample".
We didn't get a magnetic sample.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, are you going to have any weigh bags?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Yeah, we'll have some weigh bags.
(Pause while Al closes and locks the SRC) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That completes SRC-1 (means "2"); then (double checking his memory of the contents against the checklist), we have the organic control sample, 1 SESC container, four core tubes, and one bag of documented samples.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Going to the MESA) Can I fit what?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: This rock in this bag if we put it this way.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (At the MESA) I'll give it a try.
Wait for me there, just a second, while I get these in (the ETB).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (At the MESA on the right) All right.
We need the Plus Z-27 bag, right?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (At the MET on the right) Yeah.
Either that, or else put that in the weigh bag, and take this up with it.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, I'm getting you a bag for it (out of the MESA).
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, we'll use that one, then.
Here's your two weigh bags that go in the ETB.
(Going to the MESA on the left) How are you fixed for (garbled), there?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: We'll only have to make two trips.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (going around to the left side of the MESA) Okay.
Let me...(put these) two babies right here, so we don't lose them.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'll put that in the weigh bag on the next trip.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: That can just be a separate trip by itself.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, you'd better hold it up.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Now, have you got everything, Al?
Got all the others in here?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Going over to the MET) Yeah, let me do one more check here.
See if we got some more in this bag.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: These weigh bags are going to be...You're going to make a separate trip out of them, huh?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I guess we'll have to, Ed.
(Pause) I sure can't get it in there, now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Fredo, how much time have we got?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We should be in pretty good shape.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (At the MESA on the right) Houston, how much time do we have left?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Going to the MESA on the left) That do it...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: ...we've got about 18 minutes, now (to cabin repress).
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Oh, we've got lots of time.
(To Al, who has turned toward the MET.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Looking at his checklist) You have the...(The) ETB's stowed, right?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Now, let's see what we got left.
(Pause as Ed gets set) There's the greatest javelin throw of the century!
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Right in the middle of the crater.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, we missed one there, didn't we?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Put that in the weigh bag.
(Pause as Ed takes the sample to the MESA)
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Ed, I didn't hear the solar wind called-off there.
Did you get that one stowed?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (At the MESA on the right) Yup.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Going to the MESA on the left) Okay.
We'll just have these three weigh bags, then.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, did the 100-foot tether also get into the ETB?
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Both back at the MET) Okay; we'll take those along.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: How're we going to handle them?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Both back at the MESA) I'll put them in here next trip
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: And, Al and Ed, I just wanted to check once again on the camera mags to make sure you got four 70's and four 16-millimeter mags.
I guess one of the 70's is on a camera.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Yup; there's nothing left on the MET.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think we've cleaned it off.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Both back at the MESA) Want to head on up the ladder?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I believe if you just stomp your feet on the way up, it'll be as effective as the brush was yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (At the foot of the ladder) Did you...I saw you over here.
Did you get a picture (of Earth)?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Of the LM in the foreground?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, you ready to go up?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: All right, Fredo, I'm starting up the ladder.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Stomping his boots on the ladder) How's that doing?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Shaking the heck out of the LM.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: ...someone must have got caught in the cable; we just saw the TV go over.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Well, we finally did it to you; sorry.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'll go set it...I'll go set it back up again.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Exaggerated speech) Fix up the television camera.
(Long Pause as Al rights the camera and uses a
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Aim it back at the LM.
Do you see anything at all?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I think it's a better picture.
Lunar dust helps the TV picture, I guess.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: (Laughs) Okay, we'll see to it that all TV lenses get dusted in the future.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Cuts you down four stops, Fred.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: You just about had it centered, there.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Did you see that mighty leap, Fredo?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Ed, you can start on up, now.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: If I get some of my visors up here so I can see.
(Pause as Ed hops part way down the ladder) How far back do I have to look (for Earth)?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Oh, I'm looking the wrong way.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: We got two loads of the ETB.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Climbing up to the porch) Just had to have a quick look at Earth from the...
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Well, I got some pictures of the LM in the foreground; so, hope it comes out all right.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Pretty small sliver left, isn't it?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, why don't you take the first ETB as soon as you're ready; then, we can run the tracker light thing in between.
(Pause; still at the MESA) Okay, standing by.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Houston reads you loud and clear.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Yeah, I read you, Al...(correcting himself) Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, I'm ready to bring it up.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Moving into the LM shadow to get into position directly west of the ladder) Okay, stand by.
I'm going to get around a little bit more, here.
(Long Pause as the ETB goes up to the cabin) Very good.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Fredo, is the ALSEP antenna still doing okay?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Roger, Al; they're getting good signal.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, Al, bring it (the ETB) down.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pulling the LEC strap, hand over hand) All righty, coming back down.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Taking the ETB over to the MESA and needing a little more strap) Little more...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Long Pause as Al puts two weigh bags in the ETB) Okay, that (third weigh) bag is so big it won't go in the ETB very well; I'll just bring it up by myself.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: You ready to bring the other two up?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Long Pause as Al closes the ETB and gets into position west of the ladder) Okay, you can take the strain now, if you like.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Releasing the LEC) Okay, it's all yours.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Going to the MESA to get the remaining weigh bag) Don't I check the tracking light now, before I come up?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Got your eye bones out of the way?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Let me know when you turn it around.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (going to the foot of the ladder and looking up) Yeah, track light's working.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, Houston, crew of Antares is leaving Fra Mauro Base.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Roger, Al, you and Ed did a great job.
Don't think I could have done any better myself.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Well, I guess not now, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Haise

tokens: Okay, the dust is knocked off (of his boots).
(Long Pause as he steps up the ladder, one foot at a time)
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: (Probably on the porch) How'd you like one more bag of rocks?
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Okay, if you'll take one LEC.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: I'm running out of room in here, Al.
Take this (LEC?) while you're at it, before you come in.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The condensate tank has already been discarded, Houston.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Could you push it (the extra rock bag) a little further?
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Garbled) it up on top of the pile.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Man, the pile is high in here too.
Two ETB loads, an SRC, and an extra rock bag.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: If you're ready, get over behind the door...
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: I'm moving out of your way.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Push it down in the detent.
And, Al, it looks like there's a piece of Velcro laying right in the door.
Can you reach it before I pull the door closed?
It's one of those off the MET.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Long Pause) We have to have more door than that, Ed.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Wait a minute; back out, Al.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Pause) I keep hitting on something back here.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Yeah, you're hitting on the (DSKY) shelf.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, Houston, the door is closed.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: The Feedwater valve (on Al's suit) is Closed.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: Okay, let me go down and get the
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Why don't you just check this.
transcript.speaker: Shepard

tokens: Suit Press (means "Cabin Repress") circuit breaker coming closed.
Standing by for your O2 valve.
transcript.speaker: Mitchell

tokens: (Long Pause; the sound of repressurization stops) Okay.
transcript.speaker: Shepard
